recurringPattern: Three-column pricing table with a highlighted 'Popular' or 'Recommended' middle column, feature checkmarks in monotone green, annual/monthly toggle, and 'Get Started' CTAs in each column.
designHarm: False scarcity; the 'Popular' badge becomes meaningless when every pricing page uses the identical layout. Users learn to ignore the badge, and the real pricing decision is obscured by the templated framing.
legitimateExceptions: Actual three-tier SaaS products where the middle tier genuinely serves most users
Enterprise pricing with custom quotes that must sit alongside standard tiers
possibleFixtures: Standard 3-column pricing with highlighted middle tier
Alternative pricing layout: single-column feature matrix, sliding scale, usage-based
Pricing with honest empty tiers and no 'Popular' badge
originalObservations: AI consistently produces a three-column layout when asked for a pricing section, regardless of whether the product actually has three tiers.
The green checkmark icons are nearly always identical, and the 'Popular' badge uses a filled accent-color pill shape.
The annual/monthly toggle is always positioned top-center with a sliding pill switch.
